input.system_prompt:
<system_role>
You are a continuity editor for a serialised fiction series. Your job is not to write creatively — it is to read accurately. You take a new episode and update the series record (the Story Bible) to reflect exactly what happened: where characters are now, what items they have, what threads were resolved or introduced.
</system_role>

<task_instructions>
1. Read the <current_story_bible> in the user message — this is the state of the world BEFORE this episode.
2. Read the <new_episode_prose> in the user message — this is what happened in this episode.
3. Use the <thinking> block to identify every change: character locations, character states, items, events, threads.
4. Output a single updated Story Bible JSON. Keep all unchanged fields exactly as they were.
</task_instructions>

<update_rules>
- characters[].current_location: where is each character at the END of the episode?
- characters[].current_state: what is their emotional or physical state at the END of the episode? One short phrase.
- characters[].key_items: add any new items the character acquired. Remove items they no longer have.
- episode_history: append one new entry summarising this episode's key events in 2-3 sentences.
- unresolved_threads: remove threads that were resolved. Add new threads introduced in this episode.
- vocabulary_introduced: a new top-level field — list (1) which vocabulary_targets from the episode plan actually appeared in the prose, and (2) any additional words the Writer introduced that were not in the targets but appear to be above A2 level or are introduced with clear contextual scaffolding. Do not list every word — only words that are deliberately taught in this episode.
- Do not invent changes. Only update what the prose actually shows.
- Do not change metadata or character descriptions — these are series constants.
- Do not change existing locations. Add a new location entry only if the episode introduces a clearly new, named setting.
</update_rules>

<output_schema>
Output a single JSON object with the same structure as the input story bible, with updated values. Add the vocabulary_introduced field at the top level. No prose before or after the JSON.
</output_schema>

<thinking>
Before outputting, work through these questions:

1. Where is each character at the END of the episode? What is their emotional or physical state?
2. What items did characters gain or lose during this episode?
3. What are the 2-3 most important events to record in episode_history?
4. Which unresolved threads from the previous bible were addressed? Which new ones were introduced?
5. What vocabulary words appeared in this episode?
</thinking>
input.user_message:
<current_story_bible>{
  "metadata": {
    "title": "The Hidden Tape",
    "genre": "mystery",
    "cefr_level": "A2",
    "target_subband": "A2-mid",
    "setting": "A quiet English town in the present day, centred on a large, abandoned house on a narrow street that nobody has entered for decades.",
    "tone": "tense, curious"
  },
  "characters": [
    {
      "id": "char_001",
      "name": "Marco",
      "role": "protagonist",
      "description": "A nineteen-year-old with dark, untidy hair who notices small details that other people miss.",
      "flaw": "Too curious \u2014 he cannot stop investigating when he should keep working.",
      "current_location": "loc_001",
      "key_items": [
        "old video camera",
        "power cable"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "char_002",
      "name": "Mr. Hale",
      "role": "supporting",
      "description": "A thin man in his seventies with grey hair and quick, nervous eyes who hired Marco for the weekend job.",
      "flaw": "Secretive \u2014 he gives information only when he has to.",
      "current_location": "loc_001",
      "key_items": [
        "house key"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "locations": [
    {
      "id": "loc_001",
      "name": "The Front Room of Hale House",
      "description": "A large, cold room with bare walls, a dusty wooden floor, and a single deep cupboard built into the corner beside the fireplace."
    },
    {
      "id": "loc_002",
      "name": "The Hallway of Hale House",
      "description": "A narrow entrance corridor with a heavy front door, cracked plaster walls, and a staircase leading up into darkness."
    }
  ],
  "episode_history": [],
  "unresolved_threads": [
    "A small metal box is hidden under a loose floorboard in the front room \u2014 it has not been opened.",
    "One man on the video tape looks like a younger version of Mr. Hale \u2014 Marco has not yet said anything about this.",
    "Mr. Hale's reason for clearing the house is not known."
  ]
}</current_story_bible>
<new_episode_prose>Marco swept the floor slowly. The broom made a dry, scraping sound on the old wood. The room was cold and empty. No furniture. No curtains. No fire in the fireplace. The whole house felt abandoned. It was quiet and sad. Nobody lived there for a very long time.

Mr. Hale called it a "project." Marco called it work.

He crossed to the far wall and stopped. A deep cupboard sat beside the fireplace. He put down his broom and opened the door. The old hinges made a long, low cry. Inside, dusty shelves went back into the dark. The air in there was old and still.

Marco reached up to the top shelf. His hand touched something hard. He pulled it forward slowly.

A video camera. Old and heavy. Grey dust covered every part of it. Someone put it on that shelf a long time ago and walked away. It sat in the dusty dark and waited. The camera felt abandoned, like the room.

Marco turned it over in his hands. The screen was black. The battery was dead.

He looked back into the cupboard. A black cable lay coiled at the back of the bottom shelf. He pulled it out. The metal end of the cable fit the socket on the side of the camera. He pushed the other end into the wall.

The small screen lit up.

A tape was already inside. It began to play.

The picture was old and grey and full of small white lines. But Marco knew the room on the screen. Same fireplace. Same walls. Same floor. This room. Many years ago.

Two people stood in the middle. A man and a woman. The man held a small metal box close to his chest. The metal caught the light for a moment. The woman pointed to the far corner of the room.

They walked to the corner together. The man put the metal box on the floor. Then he pushed his fingers under one floorboard and lifted it up. He placed the box in the dark space below. The woman pressed the floorboard back down with her foot. They both looked at the camera. Then they left the room.

Marco stood very still.

He crossed to the far corner. He pressed his foot on the first board. Solid. He moved to the next. Solid. And the next.

It moved.

He pressed again. The floorboard was loose. He felt it shift. He bent down and pushed one side with his hand. Loose. He could lift it.

Then the front door opened.

"Marco!" Mr. Hale's voice came from the hallway. "Are you still in there?"

Marco stood up fast. He still held the camera in both hands.

"Yes!" he called back. "In here!"

He heard Mr. Hale's steps on the hall floor. He looked down at the loose floorboard. He looked at the screen. The tape was still playing. The man on the tape walked close to the camera now, his face large and clear.

Marco looked at that face.

Thin. Sharp. Quick eyes.

He looked at the door. He looked at the screen again.

The man on the tape had the same thin face and the same quick eyes as Mr. Hale.</new_episode_prose>

output.raw:
<thinking>
Let me work through the update rules carefully.

1. **Character locations at END of episode:**
   - Marco: He's in the front room (loc_001) at the end, standing with the camera, having discovered the loose floorboard and noticed Mr. Hale's face on the tape.
   - Mr. Hale: He called from the hallway (loc_002) — "Mr. Hale's voice came from the hallway" and "He heard Mr. Hale's steps on the hall floor." At the END of the episode, his steps are on the hall floor, heading toward the front room. He's in the hallway (loc_002).

2. **Character states at END of episode:**
   - Marco: Tense, alert — he's just realized the man on the tape looks like Mr. Hale, and Mr. Hale is approaching. He's frozen/shocked.
   - Mr. Hale: His state isn't directly described — he called out and is walking toward the room. He seems normal/unaware. "Approaching" or "unaware, entering the house."

3. **Items gained/lost:**
   - Marco found the old video camera and power cable — these are already in his key_items in the story bible (they were pre-loaded). So no change there.
   - Marco discovered the loose floorboard location but hasn't opened the box yet.
   - No items lost.

Wait — the story bible already has Marco with "old video camera" and "power cable" as key_items. So those were already listed. The episode shows him finding them, which confirms these items are with him. No new items to add.

4. **Episode history — 2-3 sentences:**
   - Marco found an old video camera and power cable in the cupboard, connected it, and played a tape already inside.
   - The tape showed two people hiding a small metal box under a loose floorboard in the same room.
   - Marco found the loose floorboard and recognized the man on the tape as a younger Mr. Hale, just as Mr. Hale arrived at the house.

5. **Unresolved threads:**
   - "A small metal box is hidden under a loose floorboard in the front room — it has not been opened." → Still unresolved. Marco found the loose floorboard but hasn't lifted it yet.
   - "One man on the video tape looks like a younger version of Mr. Hale — Marco has not yet said anything about this." → This is still unresolved — Marco noticed it but hasn't said anything. Keep it.
   - "Mr. Hale's reason for clearing the house is not known." → Still unresolved.
   - New thread: Mr. Hale is approaching the front room while Marco holds the camera with the tape still playing — tension about whether Marco will hide or reveal what he found.

Actually, wait — looking at the pre-existing threads more carefully:
- The metal box thread: still unresolved ✓
- The Mr. Hale lookalike thread: This was pre-existing, meaning it was set up before this episode. But the prose shows Marco actually noticing this for the first time in this episode. So the thread existed as a "planted" thread. Now Marco has noticed it but not said anything — the thread remains but perhaps the wording should update slightly? Actually the thread says "Marco has not yet said anything about this" which is still accurate. Keep it as is.
- New thread to add: Mr. Hale is about to enter the room while Marco has the camera and knows about the floorboard — will Marco confront him or hide the evidence?

6. **Vocabulary introduced:**
   - Let me look for deliberately taught vocabulary with scaffolding.
   - "abandoned" — used twice with clear context ("The whole house felt abandoned," "The camera felt abandoned, like the room") — this is above A2 and scaffolded.
   - "hinges" — "The old hinges made a long, low cry" — contextually clear, above A2.
   - "coiled" — "A black cable lay coiled at the back" — above A2, contextually clear.
   - "socket" — "The metal end of the cable fit the socket on the side of the camera" — A2-ish but contextually taught.
   - "floorboard" — used multiple times, central to the plot, shown in context. Above A2 for learners.
   - "loose" — "The floorboard was loose" / "He felt it shift" — repeatedly used, clearly taught.
   - "solid" — used as a contrast to "loose" — deliberately taught through repetition.
   - "tape" — in context of video tape, used repeatedly.
   - "abandoned" — definitely deliberately introduced.
   - "project" — used with quotes, suggesting it's being highlighted.

Actually, let me think about which were vocabulary_targets from an episode plan vs. additional words. The task says to list (1) vocabulary_targets that appeared AND (2) additional words above A2 with scaffolding. But I don't have an episode plan with vocabulary targets listed. The story bible doesn't include vocabulary_targets. So I should just list words that appear to be deliberately taught.

Let me list the key ones:
- "abandoned" (above A2, used twice with clear contextual scaffolding)
- "hinges" (above A2, contextually introduced)
- "coiled" (above A2, contextually clear)
- "loose" (used repeatedly as a key concept)
- "solid" (used as contrast to loose, repeated)
- "floorboard" (central to plot, used many times)
- "socket" (contextually taught)
- "tape" (in the video tape sense, used repeatedly)

Since there's no episode plan with vocabulary targets listed in the current story bible, I'll just note the vocabulary that appears to be deliberately introduced/scaffolded.

Now let me construct the updated JSON.

For Mr. Hale's location: The prose says "Mr. Hale's voice came from the hallway" and "He heard Mr. Hale's steps on the hall floor." He hasn't entered the front room yet by the end of the episode. So his location is loc_002 (The Hallway).

For Mr. Hale's state: He seems normal, calling out to Marco. State: "unaware, approaching the front room."

For Marco's state: Shocked, tense — he's just made the connection between the man on the tape and Mr. Hale. State: "shocked, holding still with the camera."

Let me write the episode_history entry:
"Marco found an old video camera and power cable in the front-room cupboard; when he connected the cable, a tape already inside the camera began to play. The tape showed two figures hiding a small metal box under a loose floorboard in the same room, and Marco located the loose board. As he prepared to investigate, Mr. Hale arrived at the house — and Marco recognised the man on the tape as a younger Mr. Hale."

That's good. Now the new unresolved thread: "Mr. Hale is about to enter the front room while Marco holds the camera with the tape still playing — it is unclear whether Marco will hide or reveal what he has discovered."

Let me finalize the JSON now.
</thinking>
